posts.title: Spring Boot Timeouts: The Production Guide for HTTP, DB, Redis, and Kafka
posts.description: A practical Spring Boot timeout guide covering server shutdown, RestClient/WebClient, database pools, PostgreSQL statement timeouts, Redis, Kafka producers/consumers, retries, circuit breakers, and production defaults.
posts.excerpt: Timeouts are one of the highest-return production settings in a Spring Boot service. Without them, slow dependencies turn into stuck threads, connection pool exhaustion, retry storms, cascading failures, and deploys that…

posts.title: Database Connection Pool Tuning: HikariCP, PostgreSQL, and Traffic Spikes
posts.description: A practical guide to tuning database connection pools for Spring Boot services: pool sizing, HikariCP settings, PostgreSQL limits, slow query impact, metrics, and production failure modes.

posts.title: Spring Boot Production Readiness Checklist: Timeouts, Pools, Health Checks, and Observability
posts.description: A practical Spring Boot production checklist covering HTTP timeouts, HikariCP, thread pools, Actuator health checks, graceful shutdown, structured logging, metrics, resilience, and deployment safety.
posts.excerpt: Spring Boot makes it easy to start a service. Production makes it clear whether the service is actually ready. A production-ready service is not just one that passes unit tests. It has bounded timeouts, sane thread pools…

posts.title: Java Concurrency Patterns: CompletableFuture, Structured Concurrency, and Thread-Safe Design

posts.excerpt: Java concurrency has three eras: raw  and  (Java 1-4), the  framework (Java 5+), and the virtual thread/structured concurrency era (Java 21+). Each era's patterns still exist in production codebases. Understanding all th…

posts.title: Java Memory Management Deep Dive: Heap, GC, and Production Tuning
posts.description: How the JVM allocates memory, how G1GC and ZGC work under the hood, heap analysis with JVM tools, and the GC tuning decisions that eliminate latency spikes in production Java services.
posts.excerpt: Java's garbage collector is the single biggest source of unexplained latency spikes in production services. A GC pause of 2 seconds is invisible in most logs but visible to every user who happened to make a request durin…

posts.title: Spring Security OAuth2 and JWT: Production Implementation Guide

posts.title: Scaling Spring Boot Applications to Handle 10 Million Daily Active Users
posts.description: A practical performance engineering guide: load balancing, horizontal scaling, database tuning, JVM optimization, autoscaling, and the observability stack to find and fix bottlenecks before they page you.

posts.title: Spring Boot Performance Tuning: From 200 to 2000 RPS
posts.description: Systematic approach to Spring Boot performance tuning. Covers connection pooling, N+1 query elimination, caching strategies, async processing, and JVM tuning to multiply throughput.
posts.excerpt: A Spring Boot application that handles 200 RPS on Day 1 often has the same underlying hardware capacity to handle 2000 RPS — the gap is how you use it. Most performance bottlenecks in Java web services follow predictable…

posts.title: Java GC Tuning: From G1 to ZGC — Eliminating Pause-Time Spikes
posts.description: Master Java garbage collection tuning. Understand G1GC, ZGC, and Shenandoah internals, diagnose GC issues from logs, and apply production tuning flags to eliminate pause-time spikes.
posts.excerpt: Garbage collection pauses are the invisible killer of Java application latency. A service with p99 latency of 20ms can suddenly spike to 500ms because GC paused all threads for a full collection. Understanding GC interna…

posts.title: Java 21 Virtual Threads: The End of Reactive Programming Boilerplate
posts.description: Java 21 virtual threads let you write simple blocking code that scales like async. Understand how they work under the hood, when to use them, and what pitfalls to avoid.

posts.title: Sync vs Async in Java: CompletableFuture, Reactive Streams, and Virtual Threads
